root_text.an6.54:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.an6.54:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā dhammiko jātibhūmiyaṁ āvāsiko hoti sabbaso jātibhūmiyaṁ sattasu āvāsesu.
root_text.an6.54:1.3: Tatra sudaṁ āyasmā dhammiko āgantuke bhikkhū akkosati paribhāsati vihiṁsati vitudati roseti vācāya.
root_text.an6.54:1.4: Te ca āgantukā bhikkhū āyasmatā dhammikena akkosiyamānā paribhāsiyamānā vihesiyamānā vitudiyamānā rosiyamānā vācāya pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:2.3: Atha ca pana āgantukā bhikkhū pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:2.4: Ko nu kho hetu ko paccayo yena āgantukā bhikkhū pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsan”ti?
root_text.an6.54:2.6: “ayaṁ kho āyasmā dhammiko āgantuke bhikkhū akkosati paribhāsati vihiṁsati vitudati roseti vācāya.
root_text.an6.54:2.7: Te ca āgantukā bhikkhū āyasmatā dhammikena akkosiyamānā paribhāsiyamānā vihesiyamānā vitudiyamānā rosiyamānā vācāya pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:3.1: Atha kho jātibhūmakā upāsakā yena āyasmā dhammiko tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ dhammikaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an6.54:3.4: Atha kho āyasmā dhammiko tamhā āvāsā aññaṁ āvāsaṁ agamāsi.
root_text.an6.54:3.5: Tatrapi sudaṁ āyasmā dhammiko āgantuke bhikkhū akkosati paribhāsati vihiṁsati vitudati roseti vācāya.
root_text.an6.54:3.6: Te ca āgantukā bhikkhū āyasmatā dhammikena akkosiyamānā paribhāsiyamānā vihesiyamānā vitudiyamānā rosiyamānā vācāya pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:4.3: Atha ca pana āgantukā bhikkhū pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:4.4: Ko nu kho hetu ko paccayo yena āgantukā bhikkhū pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsan”ti?
root_text.an6.54:4.6: “ayaṁ kho āyasmā dhammiko āgantuke bhikkhū akkosati paribhāsati vihiṁsati vitudati roseti vācāya.
root_text.an6.54:4.7: Te ca āgantukā bhikkhū āyasmatā dhammikena akkosiyamānā paribhāsiyamānā vihesiyamānā vitudiyamānā rosiyamānā vācāya pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:5.1: Atha kho jātibhūmakā upāsakā yenāyasmā dhammiko tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ dhammikaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an6.54:5.4: Atha kho āyasmā dhammiko tamhāpi āvāsā aññaṁ āvāsaṁ agamāsi.
root_text.an6.54:5.5: Tatrapi sudaṁ āyasmā dhammiko āgantuke bhikkhū akkosati paribhāsati vihiṁsati vitudati roseti vācāya.
root_text.an6.54:5.6: Te ca āgantukā bhikkhū āyasmatā dhammikena akkosiyamānā paribhāsiyamānā vihesiyamānā vitudiyamānā rosiyamānā vācāya pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:6.3: Atha ca pana āgantukā bhikkhū pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:6.4: Ko nu kho hetu ko paccayo yena āgantukā bhikkhū pakkamanti, na saṇṭhanti, riñcanti āvāsan”ti?
root_text.an6.54:6.5: Atha kho jātibhūmakānaṁ upāsakānaṁ etadahosi: “ayaṁ kho āyasmā dhammiko āgantuke bhikkhū akkosati …pe….
root_text.an6.54:6.6: Yannūna mayaṁ āyasmantaṁ dhammikaṁ pabbājeyyāma sabbaso jātibhūmiyaṁ sattahi āvāsehī”ti.
root_text.an6.54:6.7: Atha kho jātibhūmakā upāsakā yenāyasmā dhammiko tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ dhammikaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an6.54:6.8: “pakkamatu, bhante, āyasmā dhammiko sabbaso jātibhūmiyaṁ sattahi āvāsehī”ti.
root_text.an6.54:6.10: “pabbājito khomhi jātibhūmakehi upāsakehi sabbaso jātibhūmiyaṁ sattahi āvāsehi.
root_text.an6.54:7.1: Atha kho āyasmā dhammiko pattacīvaramādāya yena rājagahaṁ tena pakkāmi.
root_text.an6.54:7.2: Anupubbena yena rājagahaṁ gijjhakūṭo pabbato yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ dhammikaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an6.54:7.3: “handa kuto nu tvaṁ, brāhmaṇa dhammika, āgacchasī”ti?
root_text.an6.54:7.4: “Pabbājito ahaṁ, bhante, jātibhūmakehi upāsakehi sabbaso jātibhūmiyaṁ sattahi āvāsehī”ti.
root_text.an6.54:7.5: “Alaṁ, brāhmaṇa dhammika, kiṁ te iminā, yaṁ taṁ tato tato pabbājenti, so tvaṁ tato tato pabbājito mameva santike āgacchasi.
root_text.an6.54:8.1: Bhūtapubbaṁ, brāhmaṇa dhammika, sāmuddikā vāṇijā tīradassiṁ sakuṇaṁ gahetvā nāvāya samuddaṁ ajjhogāhanti.
root_text.an6.54:8.2: Te atīradakkhiṇiyā nāvāya tīradassiṁ sakuṇaṁ muñcanti.
root_text.an6.54:8.3: So gacchateva puratthimaṁ disaṁ, gacchati pacchimaṁ disaṁ, gacchati uttaraṁ disaṁ, gacchati dakkhiṇaṁ disaṁ, gacchati uddhaṁ, gacchati anudisaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:8.4: Sace so samantā tīraṁ passati, tathāgatakova hoti.
root_text.an6.54:8.5: Sace pana so samantā tīraṁ na passati tameva nāvaṁ paccāgacchati.
root_text.an6.54:8.6: Evamevaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, yaṁ taṁ tato tato pabbājenti so tvaṁ tato tato pabbājito mameva santike āgacchasi.
root_text.an6.54:9.1: Bhūtapubbaṁ, brāhmaṇa dhammika, rañño korabyassa suppatiṭṭho nāma nigrodharājā ahosi pañcasākho sītacchāyo manoramo.
root_text.an6.54:9.2: Suppatiṭṭhassa kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, nigrodharājassa dvādasayojanāni abhiniveso ahosi, pañca yojanāni mūlasantānakānaṁ.
root_text.an6.54:9.3: Suppatiṭṭhassa kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, nigrodharājassa tāva mahantāni phalāni ahesuṁ;
root_text.an6.54:9.7: Suppatiṭṭhassa kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, nigrodharājassa ekaṁ khandhaṁ rājā paribhuñjati saddhiṁ itthāgārena, ekaṁ khandhaṁ balakāyo paribhuñjati, ekaṁ khandhaṁ negamajānapadā paribhuñjanti, ekaṁ khandhaṁ samaṇabrāhmaṇā paribhuñjanti, ekaṁ khandhaṁ migā paribhuñjanti.
root_text.an6.54:9.8: Suppatiṭṭhassa kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, nigrodharājassa na koci phalāni rakkhati, na ca sudaṁ aññamaññassa phalāni hiṁsanti.
root_text.an6.54:10.1: Atha kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, aññataro puriso suppatiṭṭhassa nigrodharājassa yāvadatthaṁ phalāni bhakkhitvā sākhaṁ bhañjitvā pakkāmi.
root_text.an6.54:10.2: Atha kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, suppatiṭṭhe nigrodharāje adhivatthāya devatāya etadahosi:
root_text.an6.54:10.4: Yāva pāpo manusso, yatra hi nāma suppatiṭṭhassa nigrodharājassa yāvadatthaṁ phalāni bhakkhitvā sākhaṁ bhañjitvā pakkamissati, yannūna suppatiṭṭho nigrodharājā āyatiṁ phalaṁ na dadeyyā’ti.
root_text.an6.54:10.5: Atha kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, suppatiṭṭho nigrodharājā āyatiṁ phalaṁ na adāsi.
root_text.an6.54:11.1: Atha kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, rājā korabyo yena sakko devānamindo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.54:11.2: ‘yagghe, mārisa, jāneyyāsi suppatiṭṭho nigrodharājā phalaṁ na detī’ti.
root_text.an6.54:11.3: Atha kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, sakko devānamindo tathārūpaṁ iddhābhisaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkhāsi, yathā bhusā vātavuṭṭhi āgantvā suppatiṭṭhaṁ nigrodharājaṁ pavattesi ummūlamakāsi.
root_text.an6.54:11.4: Atha kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, suppatiṭṭhe nigrodharāje adhivatthā devatā dukkhī dummanā assumukhī rudamānā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.an6.54:12.1: Atha kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, sakko devānamindo yena suppatiṭṭhe nigrodharāje adhivatthā devatā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā suppatiṭṭhe nigrodharāje adhivatthaṁ devataṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.54:12.2: ‘kiṁ nu tvaṁ, devate, dukkhī dummanā assumukhī rudamānā ekamantaṁ ṭhitā’ti?
root_text.an6.54:12.3: ‘Tathā hi pana me, mārisa, bhusā vātavuṭṭhi āgantvā bhavanaṁ pavattesi ummūlamakāsī’ti.
root_text.an6.54:12.4: ‘Api nu tvaṁ, devate, rukkhadhamme ṭhitāya bhusā vātavuṭṭhi āgantvā bhavanaṁ pavattesi ummūlamakāsī’ti?
root_text.an6.54:12.5: ‘Kathaṁ pana, mārisa, rukkho rukkhadhamme ṭhito hotī’ti?
root_text.an6.54:12.6: ‘Idha, devate, rukkhassa mūlaṁ mūlatthikā haranti, tacaṁ tacatthikā haranti, pattaṁ pattatthikā haranti, pupphaṁ pupphatthikā haranti, phalaṁ phalatthikā haranti.
root_text.an6.54:12.7: Na ca tena devatāya anattamanatā vā anabhinandi vā karaṇīyā.
root_text.an6.54:12.8: Evaṁ kho, devate, rukkho rukkhadhamme ṭhito hotī’ti.
root_text.an6.54:12.9: ‘Aṭṭhitāyeva kho me, mārisa, rukkhadhamme bhusā vātavuṭṭhi āgantvā bhavanaṁ pavattesi ummūlamakāsī’ti.
root_text.an6.54:12.10: ‘Sace kho tvaṁ, devate, rukkhadhamme tiṭṭheyyāsi, siyā te bhavanaṁ yathāpure’ti.
root_text.an6.54:12.11: ‘Ṭhassāmahaṁ, mārisa, rukkhadhamme, hotu me bhavanaṁ yathāpure’ti.
root_text.an6.54:13.1: Atha kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, sakko devānamindo tathārūpaṁ iddhābhisaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkhāsi, yathā bhusā vātavuṭṭhi āgantvā suppatiṭṭhaṁ nigrodharājaṁ ussāpesi, sacchavīni mūlāni ahesuṁ.
root_text.an6.54:13.2: Evamevaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, api nu taṁ samaṇadhamme ṭhitaṁ jātibhūmakā upāsakā pabbājesuṁ sabbaso jātibhūmiyaṁ sattahi āvāsehī”ti?
root_text.an6.54:13.3: “Kathaṁ pana, bhante, samaṇo samaṇadhamme ṭhito hotī”ti?
root_text.an6.54:13.4: “Idha, brāhmaṇa dhammika, samaṇo akkosantaṁ na paccakkosati, rosantaṁ na paṭirosati, bhaṇḍantaṁ na paṭibhaṇḍati.
root_text.an6.54:13.5: Evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, samaṇo samaṇadhamme ṭhito hotī”ti.
root_text.an6.54:13.6: “Aṭṭhitaṁyeva maṁ, bhante, samaṇadhamme jātibhūmakā upāsakā pabbājesuṁ sabbaso jātibhūmiyaṁ sattahi āvāsehī”ti.
root_text.an6.54:13.7: “Bhūtapubbaṁ, brāhmaṇa dhammika, sunetto nāma satthā ahosi titthakaro kāmesu vītarāgo.
root_text.an6.54:13.8: Sunettassa kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, satthuno anekāni sāvakasatāni ahesuṁ.
root_text.an6.54:13.9: Sunetto satthā sāvakānaṁ brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desesi.
root_text.an6.54:13.10: Ye kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, sunettassa satthuno brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desentassa cittāni na pasādesuṁ te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjiṁsu.
root_text.an6.54:13.11: Ye kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, sunettassa satthuno brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desentassa cittāni pasādesuṁ te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjiṁsu.
root_text.an6.54:14.1: Bhūtapubbaṁ, brāhmaṇa dhammika, mūgapakkho nāma satthā ahosi …pe…
root_text.an6.54:18.1: … Jotipālo nāma satthā ahosi titthakaro kāmesu vītarāgo.
root_text.an6.54:18.2: Jotipālassa kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, satthuno anekāni sāvakasatāni ahesuṁ.
root_text.an6.54:18.3: Jotipālo satthā sāvakānaṁ brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desesi.
root_text.an6.54:18.4: Ye kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, jotipālassa satthuno brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desentassa cittāni na pasādesuṁ te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjiṁsu.
root_text.an6.54:18.5: Ye kho pana, brāhmaṇa dhammika, jotipālassa satthuno brahmalokasahabyatāya dhammaṁ desentassa cittāni pasādesuṁ te kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjiṁsu.
root_text.an6.54:19.2: yo ime cha satthāre titthakare kāmesu vītarāge, anekasataparivāre sasāvakasaṅghe paduṭṭhacitto akkoseyya paribhāseyya, bahuṁ so apuññaṁ pasaveyyā”ti?
root_text.an6.54:19.4: “Yo kho, brāhmaṇa dhammika, ime cha satthāre titthakare kāmesu vītarāge anekasataparivāre sasāvakasaṅghe paduṭṭhacitto akkoseyya paribhāseyya, bahuṁ so apuññaṁ pasaveyya.
root_text.an6.54:19.5: Yo ekaṁ diṭṭhisampannaṁ puggalaṁ paduṭṭhacitto akkosati paribhāsati, ayaṁ tato bahutaraṁ apuññaṁ pasavati.
root_text.an6.54:19.7: Nāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa dhammika, ito bahiddhā evarūpiṁ khantiṁ vadāmi, yathāmaṁ sabrahmacārīsu.

translation_text.an6.54:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.an6.54:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Dhammika was a resident in all seven monasteries of his native land.
translation_text.an6.54:1.3: There he abused visiting mendicants; he insulted, harmed, attacked, and harassed them.
translation_text.an6.54:1.4: The visiting mendicants who were treated in this way did not stay. They left, abandoning the monastery.
translation_text.an6.54:2.1: Then the local lay followers thought to themselves,
translation_text.an6.54:2.2: “We have supplied the mendicant Saṅgha with robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an6.54:2.3: But the visiting mendicants don’t stay. They leave, abandoning the monastery.
translation_text.an6.54:2.4: What is the cause, what is the reason for this?”
translation_text.an6.54:2.5: Then the local lay followers thought to themselves,
translation_text.an6.54:2.6: “This Venerable Dhammika abuses visiting mendicants; he insults, harms, attacks, and harasses them.
translation_text.an6.54:2.7: The visiting mendicants who were treated in this way do not stay. They leave, abandoning the monastery.
translation_text.an6.54:2.8: Why don’t we banish Venerable Dhammika?”
translation_text.an6.54:3.1: Then the local lay followers went up to Venerable Dhammika and said to him,
translation_text.an6.54:3.4: Then Venerable Dhammika left and went to another monastery.
translation_text.an6.54:3.5: There he abused visiting mendicants; he insulted, harmed, attacked, and harassed them.
translation_text.an6.54:3.6: The visiting mendicants who were treated in this way did not stay. They left, abandoning the monastery.
translation_text.an6.54:4.1: Then the local lay followers thought to themselves: …
translation_text.an6.54:5.4: Then Venerable Dhammika left and went to another monastery.
translation_text.an6.54:5.5: There he abused visiting mendicants; he insulted, harmed, attacked, and harassed them.
translation_text.an6.54:5.6: The visiting mendicants who were treated in this way did not stay. They left, abandoning the monastery.
translation_text.an6.54:6.1: Then the local lay followers thought to themselves,
translation_text.an6.54:6.6: “Why don’t we banish Venerable Dhammika from all seven monasteries in our native land?”
translation_text.an6.54:6.7: Then the local lay followers went up to Venerable Dhammika and said to him,
translation_text.an6.54:6.8: “Sir, please leave all seven monasteries in our native land.”
translation_text.an6.54:6.10: “I’ve been banished by the local lay followers from all seven monasteries in my native land.
translation_text.an6.54:6.11: Where am I to go now?”
translation_text.an6.54:6.13: “Why don’t I go to see the Buddha?”
translation_text.an6.54:7.1: Then Venerable Dhammika took his bowl and robe and set out for Rājagaha.
translation_text.an6.54:7.2: Eventually he came to Rājagaha and the Vulture’s Peak. He went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.an6.54:7.3: “So, Brahmin Dhammika, where have you come from?”
translation_text.an6.54:7.4: “Sir, I’ve been banished by the local lay followers from all seven monasteries in my native land.”
translation_text.an6.54:7.5: “Enough, Brahmin Dhammika, what’s that to you? Now that you’ve been banished from all of those places, you have come to me.
translation_text.an6.54:8.1: Once upon a time, some sea-merchants set sail for the ocean deeps, taking with them a land-spotting bird.
translation_text.an6.54:8.2: When their ship was out of sight of land, they released the bird.
translation_text.an6.54:8.3: It flew right away to the east, the west, the north, the south, upwards, and in-between.
translation_text.an6.54:8.4: If it saw land on any side, it went there and stayed.
translation_text.an6.54:8.5: But if it saw no land on any side it returned to the ship.
translation_text.an6.54:8.6: In the same way, now that you’ve been banished from all of those places, you have come to me.
translation_text.an6.54:9.1: Once upon a time, King Koravya had a royal banyan tree with five trunks called ‘Well Planted’. It was shady and lovely.
translation_text.an6.54:9.2: Its canopy spread over twelve leagues, while the network of roots spread for five leagues.
translation_text.an6.54:9.3: Its fruits were as large as
translation_text.an6.54:9.5: And they were as sweet as
translation_text.an6.54:9.7: The king and harem made use of one trunk, the troops another, the people of town and country another, ascetics and brahmins another, and beasts and birds another.
translation_text.an6.54:9.8: No-one guarded the fruit, yet no-one damaged another’s fruits.
translation_text.an6.54:10.1: Then a certain person ate as much as he liked of the fruit, then broke off a branch and left.
translation_text.an6.54:10.2: Then the deity haunting the royal banyan tree thought,
translation_text.an6.54:10.4: How wicked this person is, to eat as much as they like, then break off a branch and leave! Why don’t I make sure that the royal banyan tree gives no fruit in future?’
translation_text.an6.54:10.5: Then the royal banyan tree gave no more fruit.
translation_text.an6.54:11.1: Then King Koravya went up to Sakka, lord of gods, and said to him,
translation_text.an6.54:11.2: ‘Please good fellow, you should know that the royal banyan tree called Well Planted gives no fruit.’
translation_text.an6.54:11.3: Then Sakka used his psychic powers to will that a violent storm come. And it felled and uprooted the royal banyan tree.
translation_text.an6.54:11.4: Then the deity haunting the tree stood to one side, miserable and sad, weeping, with a tearful face.
translation_text.an6.54:12.1: Then Sakka went up to that deity, and said,
translation_text.an6.54:12.2: ‘Why, god, are you standing to one side, miserable and sad, weeping, with a tearful face?’
translation_text.an6.54:12.3: ‘Because, my good fellow, a violent storm came and felled and uprooted my home.’
translation_text.an6.54:12.4: ‘Well, did you stand by your tree’s duty when the storm came?’
translation_text.an6.54:12.5: ‘But my good fellow, how does a tree stand by its duty?’
translation_text.an6.54:12.6: ‘It’s when those who need the tree’s roots, bark, leaves, flowers, or fruit take what they need.
translation_text.an6.54:12.7: Yet the deity is not displeased or upset because of this.
translation_text.an6.54:12.8: This is how a tree stands by its duty.’
translation_text.an6.54:12.9: ‘I was not standing by a tree’s duty when the storm came and felled and uprooted my home.’
translation_text.an6.54:12.10: ‘God, if you were to stand by a tree’s duty, your home may be as it was before.’
translation_text.an6.54:12.11: ‘I will stand by a tree’s duty! May my home be as it was before!’
translation_text.an6.54:13.1: Then Sakka used his psychic power to will that a violent storm come. And it raised up that mighty banyan tree and the bark of the roots was healed.
translation_text.an6.54:13.2: In the same way, Brahmin Dhammika, were you standing by an ascetic’s duty when the local lay followers banished you from all seven of the monasteries in your native land?”
translation_text.an6.54:13.3: “But sir, how do I stand by an ascetic’s duty?”
translation_text.an6.54:13.4: “When someone abuses, annoys, or argues with an ascetic, the ascetic doesn’t abuse, annoy, or argue back at them.
translation_text.an6.54:13.5: That’s how an ascetic stands by an ascetic’s duty.”
translation_text.an6.54:13.6: “I was not standing by an ascetic’s duty when the local lay followers banished me from all seven of the monasteries in my native land.”
translation_text.an6.54:13.7: “Once upon a time, there was a teacher named Sunetta. He was a religious founder and was free of desire for sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an6.54:13.8: He had many hundreds of disciples.
translation_text.an6.54:13.9: He taught them the path to rebirth in the company of Divinity.
translation_text.an6.54:13.10: Those lacking confidence in Sunetta were—when their body broke up, after death—reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an6.54:13.11: Those full of confidence in Sunetta were—when their body broke up, after death—reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an6.54:14.1: Once upon a time there was a teacher named Mūgapakkha …
translation_text.an6.54:18.1: Jotipāla. He was a religious founder and was free of desire for sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an6.54:18.2: He had many hundreds of disciples.
translation_text.an6.54:18.3: He taught them the way to rebirth in the company of Divinity.
translation_text.an6.54:18.4: Those lacking confidence in Jotipāla were—when their body broke up, after death—reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an6.54:18.5: Those full of confidence in Jotipāla were—when their body broke up, after death—reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an6.54:19.1: What do you think, Brahmin Dhammika?
translation_text.an6.54:19.2: If someone with malicious intent were to abuse and insult these six teachers with their hundreds of followers, would they not brim with much wickedness?”
translation_text.an6.54:19.5: But someone who abuses and insults a single individual accomplished in view with malicious intent brims even more with wickedness.
translation_text.an6.54:19.7: Brahmin Dhammika, I say that any injury done by those outside of the Buddhist community does not compare with what is done to one’s own spiritual companions.
translation_text.an6.54:19.8: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an6.54:19.9: ‘We will have no malicious intent for those who we want to have as our spiritual companions.’
translation_text.an6.54:19.10: That is how you should train.
translation_text.an6.54:22.2: gone beyond the fetter of sensuality.
translation_text.an6.54:22.4: they were reborn in the realm of divinity.
translation_text.an6.54:23.4: gone beyond the fetter of sensuality.
translation_text.an6.54:23.6: they were reborn in the realm of divinity.
translation_text.an6.54:24.4: free of desire, immersed in samādhi;
translation_text.an6.54:25.3: a single individual accomplished in view,
translation_text.an6.54:25.4: a mendicant disciple of the Buddha;
translation_text.an6.54:26.1: You shouldn’t attack a holy person,
translation_text.an6.54:26.2: who has given up the grounds for views.
translation_text.an6.54:26.4: the seventh of the noble Saṅgha.
translation_text.an6.54:27.1: They’re not free of desire for sensual pleasures,
translation_text.an6.54:27.2: and their faculties are still immature:
translation_text.an6.54:28.1: If you attack such a mendicant,
translation_text.an6.54:29.4: An astute person is always uninjured.”

variant_text.an6.54:9.8: na ca sudaṁ → na ca puna (mr)
variant_text.an6.54:10.4: Yāva pāpo manusso → yāva pāpamanusso (sya-all); yāvatā pāpamanusso (mr)
variant_text.an6.54:11.3: abhisaṅkhāsi → abhisaṅkhāresi (sya-all, mr) | pavattesi → pātesi (bj, pts1ed)
variant_text.an6.54:19.9: na no samasabrahmacārīsu → na no āmasabrahmacārīsu (bj, sya-all); na no sabrahmacārīsu (si, pts1ed)
